prompt: Looking at `Hoffmann.Inventory.Data.Repositories.WarehouseRepository`. Every method is one line like `GetById(int id) => _context.Warehouses.FindAsync(id);` and `Search(WarehouseFilter f) => _context.Warehouses.Where(...).ToListAsync();`. Eight callers across the inventory service. Our team lead added it three years ago 'so we can swap EF Core later'. We never have. Worth keeping or should I have all the consumers query DbContext directly?
assertions: Applies the deletion test by name to the repository
Identifies the wrapper as shallow / pass-through / hypothetical seam
Pushes back on 'swap EF Core later' as the one-adapter / hypothetical-seam anti-pattern
Identifies the real seam location: DbContext / EF Core provider level (in-memory provider or similar)
Uses at least 3 of the skill vocabulary terms: seam, shallow, depth, leverage, locality, hypothetical, adapter
Ends with 'Which of these would you like to explore?' or equivalent prompt to user
